prompt: A surreal but physically plausible scene in a crowded urban plaza at dusk. At the center stands a single person of average size, slightly isolated, looking around. Surrounding them is a dense crowd of people, but subtly distorted: the surrounding individuals appear slightly larger, more prominent, and visually interconnected — their postures lean toward each other, forming tight clusters, while the central figure stands apart. The crowd composition creates an imbalance: wherever the viewer looks, others seem more socially engaged and connected than the central person. Groups overlap, gestures imply conversation and familiarity, while the central figure remains visually disconnected despite being surrounded. Lighting is cinematic and grounded — warm streetlights illuminating the crowd, with soft shadows and depth. The central figure is clearly visible but not highlighted artificially. No text, no symbols, no diagrams. The paradox must emerge purely from spatial relationships and composition. Ultra-realistic, natural human proportions, subtle surreal distortion, shallow depth of field, emotionally unsettling, grounded in reality but perceptually contradictory.
visual_logic: A central person appears less connected while everyone around them forms dense social clusters, making others seem more socially rich despite being part of the same network.
